source.notes:
POC focus: tool-calling into Zendesk + internal billing API for refunds and escalations. Wants idempotent create/update ticket flow and explicit success receipts for each tool-call (no silent failures).

- CTO Priya Rao: "We need a system that never drops transactions during Black Friday spikes."
- Concern: current webhook-based approach loses events when downstream is at capacity.
- Security: mandatory SAML SSO + org-level audit logs + KMS-managed keys. EU customers require residency; legal flagged model artifact handling for private deployments.
- Latency/Throughput targets: ticket creation < 200ms added latency end-to-end, 6k concurrent agent actions at peak (burst), 99.9% success under load.
- Model preferences: prefer trimmed open models with quantization for cost control; fallback to smaller model for non-sensitive paths.
- Billing: Prefers committed Dedicated capacity if unit economics clear.

Shorthand from calls: "POC: 2 weeks hosted headless then Dedicated staging"; "need playbook for rollback + retry/backoff". SE asked for specific API contract and example tool-call payloads. Legal requested a draft DPA and security questionnaire (sent).

source.use_case_summary: Primary: customer-facing chat agent (streamed responses) with high concurrency; secondary: reranking for search results and nearline document summarization. Strong cost sensitivity at scale; tail-latency is business-critical for chat UX.
source.slo_requirements:
Key SLO/measurement asks and alignment notes:
- Streaming chat (first-byte and steady-state tokens): target p50 <= 120ms, p95 <= 420ms, p99 <= 1.1s (client-observed). Must differentiate first-token latency vs per-token cadence.
- Non-streaming completions (API returns full completion): target p50 <= 350ms, p95 <= 1.6s, p99 <= 3.5s (client-observed).
- Reranking (small prompt + model scoring): target p95 <= 500ms.
- Measurement definition: CypherBridge insists on client-side wall-clock (end-to-end) timings for SLA acceptance; Redwood’s default is server-side processing times. Both teams agreed on a reconciliation plan:
  * Shared instrumentation: add a small client-side shim that emits timestamps to Redwood tracing on ingress/egress.
  * Measurement windows: 30-day rolling window for contractual SLA; 7-day warmup ignored for ramp.
  * Aggregation: report p50/p95/p99 computed on request-level end-to-end latencies, with sampling for high-volume endpoints if needed.
  * Streaming-specific: define first_byte_time (time until first token delivered) and per_token_percentile (distribution of inter-token latencies). SLOs to be evaluated on first_byte_time primarily.
  * Error handling: requests that timeout or are aborted by client excluded from SLA numerator; retried requests measured separately.
- Acceptance criteria for POC: replay of production traces with 2k RPS sustained for 1 hour with p95 of streaming first_byte <= 450ms and p99 <= 1.2s.
- Reserved capacity ask: estimate to meet targets under expected concurrency = 200 concurrent chat streams sustained, burst to 600 concurrent; initial dedicated footprint requested: 4x H100 (estimate), with plan to scale to 8x if KV cache hit < 70% under load.
- Observability: require per-route p50/p95/p99 dashboards, token-level breakdown, and request traces with correlation ids sent back to CypherBridge logs.
- Contractual SLA tweaks: CypherBridge requests credits for p99 breaches measured client-side; Redwood proposes hybrid: server-side breach triggers investigation with credits only if client-side confirmatory signals show network not at fault.

source.notes:
-- Company background:
- Seeded in 2020, builds a GitHub-style PR review assistant used by internal engineering and SRE teams.
- Focus: automated diff summarization, actionable comment generation, reranking candidate comments based on code context and style guides.

-- Primary ask for Redwood:
- Low-latency token-streaming inside IDE (VS Code extension) for near-real-time comment suggestion (target p95 < 100ms for token chunks).
- Deterministic PR summaries for CI checks (model/version pinning + reroll strategy).
- Reranking service for top-3 comment candidates, with adjustable scoring weights (security, style, performance).
- Support for multi-model routing: small fast model for short diffs, larger model for complex refactors.

-- Security & deployment constraints:
- Must run within VPC for customer repos that contain PII/credentials; enterprise customers require private deployment option.
- SSO via SAML, audit logging retained 1 year, KMS-managed keys for encryption, SOC2 compliance required.
- Data residency: EU customers require EU-only hosting.

-- Timeline / recent activities:
- 2026-02-11: Kickoff call (Fireflies ff_2026-02-11_DevReviewKickoff). AE Maya + SE Ethan joined. Demo of competitor Copilot integration.
- 2026-02-18: Sent initial security questionnaire (drive:/deals/devreview/pr-technical-reqs.md).
- 2026-03-02: POC checkpoint call (Fireflies ff_2026-03-02_POC-Checkpoint). Confirmed GitHub App webhook flow and sample repo.
- 2026-03-05: Engineering asked for sample latency traces and kernel selection notes (email thread_devreview_sales_20260210).
- POC milestones (customer-specified):
  * Week 1: GitHub App + VS Code extension integration, basic streaming enabled.
  * Week 2: Diff summarization accuracy run on 500 repos, measure F1 vs human summaries.
  * Week 3: Security review and penetration checklist (legal + infra).
  * Week 4: Scale test (200 concurrent reviewers, 300 req/s sustained) and cost estimate.

-- Technical preferences / details captured:
- Preferred model types: quantized LLaMA-family or Llama2-variants for balance of latency and quality. Customer asked about Redwood's verified model catalog and recommended quantization profiles.
- Model pinning required per-repo and per-branch (must support rollout/canary to 5% of traffic).
- Caching: aggressive prefix/KV cache for long-running threads; needs hinting APIs for repo-specific caches.
- Reranking: wants ability to plug in custom heuristics (security scanner scores) into rerank pipeline.

-- Quote from Eng Lead (paraphrased): "We need deterministic outputs for CI commentary — if the bot comments wrongly it blocks merges. Also want a fast fallback path so reviewers aren't blocked if model is busy."

-- Commercial notes:
- Interested in Dedicated for predictable latency with the option to move to Private for EU-only enterprise customers.
- Budget: internal estimate $50k-150k ARR depending on scale and retention.

-- Next steps / ask list:
- Provide Dedicated trial access and a sandboxed GitHub App for POC.
- Security deep-dive: SOC2 report + KMS integration docs.
- Latency benchmark runbook and recommended kernel/quantization settings for their workload.
- Legal to review data residency clause; request sample contract language.

-- Internal AE notes:
- AE Maya to coordinate legal doc handoff. SE Ethan to run two targeted latency tests week of 2026-03-15. Follow-up scheduled 2026-03-18.

source.notes:
Top-line: mid-market billing SaaS (B2B) scaling support operations. Evaluating Redwood Hosted API as an initial POC to replace brittle webhook/rule stacks for subscription support agents. Plan: 2-week POC on Hosted API focusing on streaming chat latency and reliable function/tool-calling into Stripe sandbox. If POC proves latency and cost targets, move to Dedicated for predictable monthly throughput.

Key technical requirements (customer language / AE notes):
- Workload: customer support agents handling billing/subscription flows (refunds, invoice re-creation, plan changes). Heavy use of tool-calling to Stripe/Zuora sandbox; outputs must be strict JSON for automated downstream processing.
- Latency SLO: time-to-first-byte (streaming start) < 200ms for agent-facing flows; end-to-end JSON payload generation for tool call within 500ms median under 30 concurrent sessions.
- Throughput: expect bursts ~30-60 concurrent agent sessions during EMEA morning; monthly token estimate ~500k-1.2M tokens with heavy peaks during billing cycles.
- Cost sensitivity: wants unit economics scenarios for (a) Hosted with autoscaling vs (b) Dedicated reserved pools + burst pricing. Needs example invoice showing cost per 10k support interactions.
- Model preferences: prefer Redwood-curated open models with fallback to smaller quantized variants for low-risk flows (billing FAQs) and larger variant for complex disputes. Routing rules: latency-first for live agent flows, cost-first for async triage.
- Security/compliance: SSO/SAML required; audit logging and KMS for keys; data residency (EU for EU customers) important for regulated clients.

POC scope (agreed on 2026-03-11):
- Week 1: Setup Hosted API access, connect Stripe sandbox, implement simple function calling endpoint that accepts JSON schema: {action, invoice_id, amount_cents, reason, idempotency_key}. Validate idempotency and error modes. SE to provide sample code.
- Week 2: Simulated agent traffic (30 concurrent) with streaming responses; measure TTFB, p50/p95 token latencies, model cost per session. Collect transcripts and structured outputs.
- Success criteria: TTFB < 200ms p50; function call JSON schema matches spec 95% of the time; cost per 10k interactions within agreed threshold; no data leakage in logs; SSO demoable in staging.

Quotes / call notes: "If we can't get first-byte that fast we can't go live — agent UX will suffer" (Head of Support). "We need concrete cost numbers for peak month before procurement can sign" (VP Finance).

Next steps (short):
- Schedule deep-dive POC kickoff w/ infra and security (owner Jordan + SE Priya)
- Provide dedicated sizing worksheet and example Hosted vs Dedicated pricing scenario (AE to attach deck)

source.notes:
Summary: Auraborne is an adtech SSP/RTB stack (header bidding + real-time personalization). They need sub-50ms p95 end-to-end inference for impression-time scoring and reranking, with peak bursts to ~6k rps and sustained 1.2k rps. Very cost-sensitive: want to evaluate batching, KV/prefix caching, and aggressive quantization to reduce GPU count and TCO.

Key requirements (from AE/SE calls):
- p95 <= 50ms for scoring path; target 30ms for experiments.
- Throughput: baseline 1.2k rps, burstable to 6k rps for 2-5s windows.
- Deterministic fallbacks: fallback routing to smaller quantized model when Dedicated pool saturated.
- Cold-start behavior: minimal latency for first request after idle (we discussed warm pools + micro-batching).
- Model preferences: want to test Llama2-13B-quant, Mistral-7B-8bit, and Redwood-verified 7B variants.
- Data constraints: ad payloads include user IDs (hashed). Must remain in customer VPC; logs retention 90d; require KMS integration + audit log export.

Timeline & recent activity:
- 2026-01-20: Initial inbound demo request via website (AE: Maya assigned).
- 2026-02-03: Intro call (Maya/Ethan) — discovery; high-level perf targets captured. (ff_20260203_auraborne_intro)
- 2026-02-18: Security questionnaire submitted; SEC ticket opened (SEC-451).
- 2026-03-03: Detailed architecture review + perf expectations (SE deep-dive). Fireflies: ff_20260303_auraborne_1.
- 2026-03-08: Submitted POC scope doc + pricing (drive:/deals/auraborne/Redwood_POC_pricing_v2.pdf).
- 2026-03-11: Contract/legal flagged data residency clause; procurement loop started.

POC plan (agreed, 4 weeks):
- Week 0: Access & baseline: deploy Dedicated pool in us-east-1 VPC, ingest sample RTB logs (~24h). Run baseline Llama2-13B float model.

source.notes:
Summary: mid-market SaaS (customer support KB) evaluating Redwood hosted API for an embeddings + ANN frontend and cross-encoder reranker. Priority = high QPS + predictable cost.

Context and needs:
- Indexing scale: ~1.0-1.4M canonical docs (mix of articles, transcripts), ~20M embedding vectors expected after passage-splitting. Daily delta ~3-5k docs.
- Query profile: customer-facing help widget + internal agent assist. Peak QPS target 500-650 qps, baseline ~250-350 qps. 95th-tail SLA request: sub-150ms response for retrieval-only; end-to-end (retrieval + rerank) target <350ms.
- Cost sensitivity: want working cost model. Ballpark target <$0.001 per served user query (prefer ~ $0.0005 - $0.0008) including embedding retrieval + rerank.
- Quality: first-pass ANN recall top-100 then cross-encoder rerank for final top-5. Need support for dense retrieval + on-the-fly reranking.
- Feature asks: incremental index updates, TTL/freshness tuning, partial residency (EU subset), request-level audit logging, SSO (SAML) for admin console, KMS integration for keys.

Timeline / recent activity:
- 2025-11-12: Intro call (AE Ethan) — high-level product fit, asked for rough perf numbers and pricing.
- 2025-12-05: Architecture deep-dive with SE (Rina) — validated pipeline idea (ANN -> cross-encoder). Action: Redwood to propose POC config.
- 2026-01-18: Security screening kickoff — customer provided compliance checklist. Legal: wants SOC2 in writing + data residency options.
- 2026-02-04: Internal sizing workshop — AxiomFlux estimated 1.2M docs; Rina suggested batching + prefix caching.
- 2026-03-03: Demo of hosted API (small sample) — customer liked latency and SDKs but asked for scaled run. Fireflies: ff_2026_3249_axiomflux_meeting.
- 2026-03-10: Pricing scoping call — customer requested a cost model for 300 qps and for a 600 qps bursting scenario.

POC ask / success criteria:
- Redwood runs a perf POC on hosted API using a representative 1M-doc index (customer uploads sanitized sample).
- Measure: retrieval-only (ANN) 95th < 150ms at 500 qps; retrieval+rerank (top-100 -> cross-encoder) end-to-end <350ms at 300 qps.
- Cost calc delivered: $/query breakdown (embeddings + ANN + reranker) and optimization recommendations (quantization, batching, cache TTLs).

Customer quotes and sentiment:
- "We need the widget to feel instant for customers but can move heavy reranking to agent side if tails don't behave."
- Product lead: "If you can show <0.8ms/k token cost improvement w/ caching & batching we'll move forward." (paraphrase)

Technical notes / constraints:
- Preferred models: open-weights embedding model + a cross-encoder for rerank (customer open to Redwood model recommendations).
- They prefer to keep documents indexed in public cloud (AWS) but require EU residency for EU-customer content.
- PII scrub pipeline planned; legal will require proof of audit logging and retention controls.

Next steps (short):
1) Schedule hosted API perf POC run window (Rina + infra team) — target week of 2026-03-24.
2) Send tailored pricing deck + breakdown by component (embeddings, ANN, rerank). Link: /drive/pricing/axiomflux-pricing-deck-v2.pdf.
3) Respond to security checklist w/ SOC2 evidence and KMS integration notes — attach security FAQ (https://drive.redwood.com/security/axiomflux-security-faq.pdf).

Open blockers (detail):
- Legal: confirmation of SOC2 scope and timeline for attestation (customer legal requires before signing).
- Residency: need a plan for partial EU-only index + confirm data egress controls.
- Perf/cost proof: customer wants measured POC results at 1M docs at representative QPS before committing to dedicated capacity.

Notes on engagement tone: ongoing, pragmatic. Customer likes engineering-first conversations. Not price-led only; performance and controls are decision drivers.

Attachments & refs: pricing deck, security FAQ, Fireflies transcript id ff_2026_3249_axiomflux_meeting, Jira RWD-11234 (request to scale test infra).

source.notes: Summary: Enterprise private (VPC) deployment use-case: low-latency inference for capital-markets support platform; must satisfy strict EU data residency and auditability. Primary asks: 1) SSO/SAML integration with Okta, group-to-role RBAC mapping in Redwood Console/control plane; 2) SCIM provisioning so HR/Identity can auto-provision/deprovision users and groups; 3) KMS integration (customer-managed keys, key rotation, optional HSM) and ability to encrypt model cache and at-rest blobs; 4) Audit logging forwarded to their SIEM (Splunk) with retention controls; 5) Private control plane reachable only over peered VPC or VPN, no internet-exposed control endpoints.
source.notes_continued:
CRM-style call notes and shorthand below:
- 2026-02-18: Security lead (Marta Koenig) said 'no hosted control plane unless it's behind our peering' -> strong VPC-only requirement.
- 2026-03-09: Okta admin (Simon): SCIM schema mismatch on group.displayName vs group.name; want wildcard mapping to console-role.
- KMS: they use Azure Key Vault and require envelope encryption with CMKs; asked if Redwood supports Azure KV + HSM-backed keys or BYOK flow.
- Audit: need S3-compatible log export + CloudWatch/Splunk forwarder and enterprise retention (7+ years for some records).
- RBAC: need at least three roles pre-baked (admin/operator/auditor) and custom role templates for team-level scoping.
- POC scope: small cluster (n-2 model versions), SCIM sync of 5 groups, SAML SSO test, KMS encrypt/decrypt test, log pipeline to demo Splunk index.
- Quote: buyer asked for 'explicit auditable actions per token, per model' for compliance reports.
- Drive refs: see drive:/security/banyan-redwood-sso-scim-questions.docx for mapping table (attached).
- Jira: RED-8421 created to track KMS support questions; RED-9175 for SCIM provisioning failures in test environment.
- Next: deliver SAML attribute mapping matrix + minimal network diagram by 2026-03-20.
- Sales note: cost sensitivity moderate; primary constraint is security/compliance, not unit cost.

source.notes:
Summary: Mid-market SaaS (product analytics) — looking to replace slow in-house reranker and move support agent → semantic doc-search. Started on Redwood Hosted API; results promising for recall but unit cost and rate limits are a concern for scaled product usage. Team prefers to evaluate Dedicated (reserved GPU pool) to get predictable throughput and lower per-token costs.

Key POC asks / technical requirements:
- Workload: hybrid (real-time chat/support-agent latency sensitive ~ < 300ms p95; batch reranking for search results at 200 qps peak).
- Model preference: prefer curated open models; want a quantized 13B/7B variant option (Llama-like 13B-quant / 7B-quant) for cost-performance tradeoffs.
- Routing/fallback: POC must include canary rollout (5% -> 25% -> 100%), plus automatic fallback to Hosted API for overflow or regional outage. They want documented rollback triggers (latency > 350ms p95, error rate > 1% or quality regression on eval set).
- Regions: primary US-West, failover EU-West for users in EMEA, data residency for EU customers must stay in EU region.
- Security: SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS integration for keys, retention controls (30/90 day retention policies).
- Networking: VPC peering required for Private control plane; egress restrictions in some customer tenants.
- Observability: token-level cost breakdown, per-route latency, and sample outputs for QA.

POC plan (agreed):
1) Week 0: infra prep — provision Dedicated pool (2xA10 equivalent), VPC peering, service account and KMS test.
2) Week 1: Deploy model variants (13B-quant as primary, 7B-quant as cheap fallback), enable KV prefix cache for support flows.
3) Week 2: Canary traffic (5%) routed from production via feature flag; routing policy: route based on tenant and latency SLO preference. Collect eval metrics + human eval on sample prompts.
4) Week 3: Increase canary (25%), run cost/perf analysis and run rollback tests (simulated GPU outage) — verify region failover to EU and fallback to Hosted API.
5) Week 4: Finalize SLOs, document runbook for rollback and capacity alerts, pricing/commercial term negotiation.

Stated concerns from Eng/Product: "We need deterministic cost numbers before committing"; "If the Dedicated pool degrades, must not break customer-facing chat - fallback must be seamless".

Artifacts / references: Fireflies call FF-12903 (design session), pricing deck in Drive, security questionnaire (link below). SE notes: Lina to provide canary routing example policy and a JSON of rollback thresholds.

source.notes:
Summary: mid-market SaaS (product analytics) building a contextual support + doc search assistant. Narrowing path: start Hosted API to validate feature, then shift to Dedicated for predictable throughput and cost. Team is cost-sensitive and cares about p95 latency and unit economics.

Recent convo highlights:
- CTO (R. Meyer): "We need consistent 50ms p95 for UI snappiness; can tolerate quantization if quality stays within threshold."
- PM: concerned about monthly token bill spikes, asked for batching/caching levers and concrete cost model.
- SE asked: prefer models with KV cache friendly profiles; want recommendations for quantization profiles that preserve entity extraction accuracy.

POC details:
- Workload: user-facing chat + background reranker (embeddings) for doc search. Peak: 220 RPS during internal load tests; expected steady 40-70 RPS.
- Targets: p95 < 60ms (interactive), 95% of queries served under 80ms. Throughput budget: sustained 100k tokens/day.
- Initial model: Llama-2 style open model variant (customer-preferred), fallback to smaller quantized variant for low-priority queries.

Cost optimization workstream (what we ran / proposed):
- Batching: showed 2.1x token throughput improvement in hosted POC with small batching window (10-20ms). Tradeoff: adds 6-10ms median latency, reduces token cost ~28%.
- Caching: prefix + KV caching for FAQ-like queries cut repeated tokens by ~18% on their corpus; recommended TTL buckets by query type.
source.additional_notes:
- Quantization profiles: tested int8 + dynamic 4-bit for reranking; engineering flagged minor degradation on entity recall for 4-bit -> need eval prompts.
- Unit economics: built a per-route model (support chat vs background embedding) showing dedicated baseline reduces per-token cost by ~35% at commit levels of 0.5-1.0M tokens/mo.
- Workshop agenda (from 2026-03-02): 1) review POC telemetry; 2) batching/caching knobs + demo; 3) quantization matrix and accuracy checks; 4) billing model and committed tiers; 5) next steps for Dedicated pilot.

Action items (open):
- Redwood: deliver Dedicated test config + pricing sheet with burst and committed tiers (Priya -> INF-3421).
- Copperlark Eng: run evaluation prompts against 4-bit quantized variant, report delta in F1/recall (due 2026-03-16).
- Legal: finalize mutual NDA and SOW template (blocker for procurement).

Stated blockers: procurement requires full quote including burst pricing, security wants SOC2 in place for Dedicated, eng needs accuracy baseline for quantized model before signing commit.

Tone / next steps: conversational, technically deep. AE to push updated Dedicated quote and schedule a 90-min follow-up focused on quantization accuracy + pricing.

source.notes:
- Vertical: enterprise SaaS KB for support teams. Moderate index size (1-1.5M docs ingest). High QPS expectation: target 300-600 qps steady-state for web-facing search.
- Primary ask: embeddings + reranking pipeline; low tail latency for top-K results (p95 < 200ms desired), keep cost-per-query < $0.005 if possible.
- Prefer hosted API (fast time-to-value) but want clear path to VPC/private later.
- Models: prefer small+fast embedding model (128-256 dim) + cross-encoder reranker for top-50 re-rank. Interested in Redwood "Optimize" suggestions (batching, cache, quantization).
- Security: SOC2 required, SSO/SAML mandatory, audit logs + KMS for encryption at rest. Data residency for EU accounts is a future requirement.
- Quote from CTO on call: "We need search that feels instant for customers — 150ms or bust for most queries. Cost needs to scale predictably."
- POC plan: index 1.2M docs (manual + HTML parsing pipeline), run simulated traffic 500 qps, compare vector DB pairing options (Pinecone vs. Milvus) and reranker compute costs.
- Internal notes: engineering liked Redwood runtime demo but asked for concrete kernel/quantization tradeoffs. SE to provide token-level cost breakdown and suggested batching thresholds.
- Risk: reranker compute dominates cost at high QPS; want fallback policy (approximate rerank or light reranker) to save $ during heartbeats.
- Next meeting: POC kickoff + security sync (2026-03-15).

notes: Summary: mid-market PM analytics SaaS (B2B) evaluating Hosted API as quick win then Dedicated for predictable cost and VPC later.
Primary workload: embeddings + vector search for product content (~2.2M total docs, incremental daily delta ~12k).
Throughput targets: sustained 80-150 QPS for semantic search; 99th pct latency target <150ms for search queries. Cost-sensitive - wants unit economics estimate for tokens + embedding unit costs.

Batch jobs: nightly full re-embed of updated docs (~2M items) must complete within 4 hours; currently takes ~10 hours on their infra. Need parallelized bulk embedding solution and native batching support.
Vector DB: currently PoC with Pinecone; evaluating Weaviate and Milvus for future self-hosted options. Wants Redwood to demonstrate direct integration patterns and connector performance.
Data residency: subset of customers in EU (requires region pinning / EU-only storage). Retention controls: 90d default, configurable per-customer.

Model preferences: prefer open/quantized models for embeddings (cost), but will accept Redwood-verified higher-quality models for top-k rerank. Interested in automatic quantization suggestions from Optimize.
Routing/fallback: important to have cheaper fallback for non-SLA routes and graceful degradation for spikes.
Security: SSO (SAML), KMS for key management, audit logging retention 1 year, incident response playbook required before Dedicated procurement.
Quote from CTO: 'We need predictable batch windows and vector DB consistency — if you can show a Dedicated config that finishes our nightly run in <4h and keeps 95th <200ms, we can sign.'

Pricing conversation: finance asked for committed pricing band for Dedicated reserved pool and an on-demand overage model. Wants example TCO comparison (Hosted vs Dedicated) for our 12-month forecast.

POC results so far: embedding throughput with Hosted API is adequate for small batches; bulk embedding API + intelligent batching from Redwood reduced time by ~30% in trial. Search latency under load still borderline with current Pinecone index settings.
Next technical ask: run a Dedicated pilot with a 2-GPU reserved pool + Redis KV cache to validate KV cache hit rate and p95 tail latency under production-shaped traffic.
Internal notes: keep pricing aggressive on first-year commit; include 2-week grace on overage meters for initial rollout.

source.notes:
POC notes / rep summary:
- Customer: Axion Reserve Labs (programmatic ad personalization). Heavy sensitivity to latency p95 and edge-case tail latency for auctions.
- Primary ask: dedicated GPU pool with predictable per-token unit economics; want a pricing model they can put into their forecasting model.
- Quote from CTO: "We need a committed plan that buys us predictable CPMs for auctions — no surprise bills mid-quarter."
- Technical constraints: VPC-only deployment, strict data residency for EU campaigns, KMS required, SOC2 evidence required for procurement.
- Traffic: peak windows (08:00-11:30 PST) with short bursts of high concurrency for auctions (spiky). Need autoscale burst capacity or fallback to hosted.
- Customer risk appetite: medium — willing to commit to 12–24 month reserved if economics clear; wants staged commit + ability to add burst credits.
- SE notes: recommend mix of 6xH100 (reserved baseline) + 2xA10G for burst/embeds to reduce unit cost; enable prefix caching for chat workflows to reduce kv hits.
- Procurement asks: committed discount tiers (15% @ 6 months, 30% @ 12 months, 35–40% @ 24 months), clear overage price-per-token, and credits for missed SLOs.
- Next: deliver 2 cost model variants (conservative util 50%, optimistic util 75%) and SLA draft. Schedule procurement sync 2026-03-18.
- Stacking artifacts: shared cost spreadsheet (drive link), sample SLA draft, POC logs attached to RI-6782.
unit_economics: Assumption A (conservative): 50% util on reserved capacity -> effective infra cost $0.0018/token (8k context avg)
Assumption B (target): 75% util -> effective infra cost $0.00095/token; with 24-month commit discount applied -> $0.00062/token
Committed discount bands tested: 12m=30% off list, 24m=40% off list; overage premium = list * 1.2
Baseline infra pricing used: H100-equivalent $2.40/hour (normalized), A10G $0.55/hour for batch embedding work
Operational adders: monitoring & retention (audit logs) estimated $1,200/mo; network egress ~ variable
reserved_capacity_plan: Proposed initial commit: 6xH100-equivalent nodes (24/7 baseline) sized for chat p95 60ms and 1500 concurrent chat sessions
Burst layer: 2xA10G nodes for embeddings and rerank spikes (auto-burst with priority routing)
KV/prefix caching: allocate 10% of RAM per model to reduce autoregressive token generation and lower per-token cost
Rollout: 30-day canary -> 90-day ramp to full traffic; monthly utilization review and quarterly price repricing clauses
sla_targets: Availability target: 99.95% (monthly) with financial credits tiered at 99.9/99.8 thresholds
Latency targets (chat): p50 < 15ms, p95 < 60ms, p99 < 120ms for 8k-context models
Throughput SLO: maintain baseline 1,500 concurrent chat sessions with burst to 4,000 for up to 5 minutes
Fallback policy: automatic routing to hosted_api model-variant in other region in case of capacity exhaustion (with transparent cost/pass-through)
Support: 24/7 on-call for Sev1 during initial 90-day rollout; dedicated TAM for contract term

source.notes:
Account summary: BeaconFlow Labs is a mid-market B2B product company building an in-app onboarding copilot (guided flows + micro-tips). Evaluating Redwood Hosted API first to prove latency/cost, then committed interest in Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation.

Key constraints / asks: 
- Latency SLOs: p50 target ~120-200ms, p95 target <500ms for interactive onboarding steps; streaming + function-calls must be consistent (users notice variance).
- Cost controls: token cost caps per onboarding session (target <= $0.15/session initially), need per-route token caps and throttles; interested in Optimize recommendations (batching/caching) to reduce unit cost.
- Tooling: must support function/tool calling to internal product metadata + CRM (tokenized access), streaming partial responses to UI while tool calls resolve.
- Security: SSO/SAML, SOC2 posture important, KMS for key management, audit logs retention policy. Data residency: US preferred for now.

POC plan: 2-week hosted run of 10k sessions simulated from production-like traffic, measure p50/p95 token usage, streaming latency, function-call end-to-end RTT. If pass, move to Dedicated quote and capacity sizing.

Call notes (shorthand):
- Elena: "Users drop off when assistant pauses > 500ms" -> strong need for consistent tail latency.
- SE (Ravi): suggested prefix/KV caching for repeated onboarding flows; showed batching configs.
- Procurement: price sensitivity on per-token for high-volume onboarding; wants Dedicated uplift only if unit economics improve or SLAs locked.

Linked artefacts: pricing deck, hosted-vs-dedicated TCO sheet, security FAQ, Fireflies transcripts (see linked_fireflies).

Next immediate actions: send hosted POC credentials, provide sample streaming config and recommended token caps per flow, schedule weekly POC syncs.

source.notes:
Customer: mid-market SaaS for customer support analytics. Current search stack: BM25 + embeddings (in-house). Pain: poor precision at top-k causing low deflection and higher agent workload. Evaluation path: hosted API first -> POC offline rerank on 50k queries -> small online A/B -> pricing discussion for dedicated if cost/throughput warrants.

POC details and asks:
- They want to use our rerank endpoint (offline scoring CSV) for head-to-head with their current ranker.
- Need clear instructions for eval set formatting (we provided template).
- Latency constraint: 95th pctl for single rerank RPC <200ms; acceptable to batch for bulk offline scoring.
- Cost sensitivity: targeting <$0.20 per user-active-month equivalent; looking to model unit economics for hosted vs dedicated.
- Routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to smaller model in case of capacity spike; also region-aware routing (US primary, EU data residency for some customers).

Security/compliance:
- VPC preferred, SOC2 required, SSO/SAML, audit logs, KMS key management. Data residency: EU for certain enterprise customers.

Quotes / paraphrases:
- PM: 'We need a measurable bump in precision@5 without blowing up latency or cost.'
- Eng lead: 'If dedicated yields predictable throughput and cost parity, we'll seriously consider committed capacity.'

Next tactical items (short):
- SE to share dedicated pricing sheet + committed-capacity scenarios (by 2026-03-12).
- AE to coordinate legal for DPA and KMS questions.
- SE running extra offline runs to produce recall/precision curves per model variant (embedding + rerank model combos).

Notes: customer likes the offline scoring workflow; wants clearer documentation on canary A/B and rollback for Dedicated. Important to capture and surface token-level cost for rerank flows in Redwood Console for their finance team.

source.summary: Enterprise fintech evaluating Redwood Dedicated for customer-facing chat and reranking. Primary constraints: strict latency tails for interactive workflows, on-prem/VPC requirement for custody of PII, cost sensitivity across peak batch inference for nightly risk models.
recent_activities: 2026-02-12: Intro call (AE + SE). Discussed high-level capacity needs and security posture.
2026-02-14: Tech deep-dive (Priya). Measured baseline with toy model; agreed to run Redwood-provided load test.
2026-02-25: Fireflies transcript review; CTO asked about p99 measurement method (ff_20260225_8811).
2026-03-03: Network and infra discovery with infra team — VPC peering constraints documented.
2026-03-09: Pricing + reserved GPU sketch reviewed; legal requested SLA drafts.
technical_notes: Primary SLO ask from CTO: 'interactive chat p99 <= 300ms client-observed for short prompts' — needs alignment on definition

We pushed back: client-observed vs server-side. Redwood suggests server-side p95/p99 measured at model runtime + queueing; client-side includes network and SDK overhead. Need to pick which party measures for the SLA.

Streaming vs non-streaming: Axial wants streaming for typing UX. Must agree how p50/p95/p99 are computed for streams (per-final-token latency vs time-to-first-token). Suggested definitions: time-to-first-token measured p50/p95; per-complete-response tail measured p99 for non-streaming endpoints.
Measurement windows: agreed 24h windows with 7-day rolling retention for reporting, plus peak-hour (5-minute) spikes for burst analysis.
Sampling strategy: production sampling vs synthetic load. Customer insists on production-sampled metrics for SLA but accepts Canary synthetic tests to validate config and reserve planning.
Tail amplification: discuss queueing effect — p99 must include queuing delays under reserved capacity; Redwood proposes separate 'queue-time' metric and combined 'end-to-end server latency' for SLOs.

Reserved GPU planning: initial sizing sketch = 8x A100-equivalents to meet 100 qps sustained for interactive chat (avg 40 tokens per response, kv-cache warm). Need capacity for 2x burst and 3x nightly batch window for reranking jobs.
Graceful degradation: proposed automatic fallback to smaller model family and degraded SLO tiers during capacity-pressure events; customer requires explicit fallbacks in SLA with rollback policy.
Monitoring hooks: Axial requires token-level cost and per-route p95 breakdown + tracing for 1% of sampled requests; Redwood can expose route-level dashboards and span-level traces.
Security notes: KMS/HSM integration questions remain (encryption at rest for KV cache snapshots). Security team asked for an architecture diagram before sign-off.
Quote from CTO (K. Morgan): 'We will accept a measured server-p99 if we can independently verify client-side timing for top routes.'
requirements_summary: Workloads: low-latency chat (interactive), reranking for search, nightly embedding pipelines.
Latency targets: target p50 40-60ms, p95 120-180ms, p99 <= 300ms for short chat prompts (client-observed goal).
Streaming: must support time-to-first-token < 120ms p95; per-token steady-state <= 40ms p95.
Throughput: sustained 100 qps for chat; spikes up to 300 qps for flash events; nightly batch/throughput for reranking ~10k QPS over scheduled windows.
Availability/SLA: enterprise-grade SLA requested (99.9% uptime) with credits and escalations.
Data controls: VPC-only control plane, ability to bring on-prem variant for regulatory customers, strict retention controls and data residency in NA region.
Observability: per-route p50/p95/p99, queue-time, time-to-first-token, client vs server timers, and 7-day metrics retention.
Compliance: SOC2 + ISO27001 required; want evidence/certs in drive doc.
Cost sensitivity: Prefers reserved committed pricing with burst buckets; wants unit economics per-1k tokens and per-embedding batch run.
Legal asks: explicit fallback behavior, incident notification SLA, and SLO measurement definition in contract.
Measurement alignment asks: definition of latency windows, whether p99 is computed across all requests or per-route, and treatment of retry amplification.
poa_next_steps: Redwood: produce SLO/measurement draft that clearly defines server-side vs client-side metrics and streaming semantics (Due: 2026-03-15).
Axial: provide representative production traffic trace (sample) to run against Redwood test harness (Due: 2026-03-19).
SE (Priya) to run dedicated load test with proposed reserved config (8x GPU sketch) and return dashboard screenshots.
Finance: finalize committed pricing worksheet and burst credit model; include ARR impact scenarios.
Legal: exchange draft SLA with explicit measurement clauses; schedule redlines session week of 2026-03-22.
Security: deliver KMS/HSM integration diagram and red team checklist for on-prem rollout.
Follow-up meeting scheduled: 2026-03-18 10:00 PT (AE + SE + CTO + Infra).
Blocker resolution: infra to confirm VPC peering egress rules before on-prem test.
If metrics align, move to procurement stage Q2 2026.
Note: customer expects independent verification step — provide lightweight instrumentation SDK example for client-side timing capture.
internal_notes: High strategic value: large fintech account, multiple product lines for Redwood (Dedicated + Private + Optimize).
Win condition: clear, contractable latency definition and predictable committed pricing with burst allowance.
Risk: CTO's p99 client-observed ask is aggressive; must negotiate to server-side p99 + client-observed 'experience' objective with joint runbook.
SE take: prioritize demonstrating stable p99 under realistic KV cache warm scenarios; illustrate fallback behavior in test harness.
Pricing note: midpoint ARR looks like $1.5M; include 12 vs 36 month commit tradeoffs.
Action: Priya to prepare runbook for 'how p99 is computed' including examples for streaming vs non-streaming.
Jira references: map RED-4321 to KMS question; RED-4478 to load-test failures during noisy neighbor scenario.
Keep CSM in loop early to prepare onboarding/observability pack if deal closes.
Remember to attach recorded Fireflies to CRM record after next meeting.
AE comment: 'Customer is technical and pragmatic — if we give unambiguous metrics and a verification path, deal accelerates.'

source.notes:
Top-line: building a VS Code copilot extension (Electron + webview) targeted at SMB developer teams. Needs sub-50ms streaming token latency for inline suggestions; users expect immediate character-by-character streaming. Current prototype uses OpenAI stream but hitting rate limits and jitter. Want model pinning per-deployment (dev/demo/prod) and ability to hot-swap model versions without redeploying extension.

Key quotes: "We need streaming to feel native — no 300ms stalls when you type." — CTO Maya Chen.

Auth: plan to use OAuth + short-lived ephemeral keys issued by our backend. Concern: Redwood's rate-limiting semantics and per-user quotas; need guidance on token refresh and graceful fallback when hitting limits.

source.next_step: Finish Hosted API POC results + pricing estimate for Dedicated commitment; schedule internal security PO review
source.notes:
Account background: Cinderpath is a mid-market B2B product analytics SaaS (user-facing product assistant + embedded code-assist feature). Currently using a major LLM API provider for chat/code assistant and embeddings. They have heavy token volume in user interactive chat (multi-turn) and periodic bulk reranking jobs for search.

Why Redwood: cost predictability at scale, better latency tail for their EU/NA customers, and desire for a single vendor that supports hosted then dedicated migration with minimal app changes. They asked specifically about Redwood's compat layer and prompt parity guarantees — worried about regressions when switching models/endpoints.

POC summary (Hosted API):
- Goals: 1) Verify prompt parity (no quality regressions), 2) Measure 95/99 latency and avg token cost, 3) Validate embedding similarity parity for product search.
- Setup: ported 3 canonical prompts + evaluation harness to Redwood Hosted endpoints; run A/B against existing provider over 48h window.
- Metrics being tracked: top1 ambiguity rate, hallucination incidents per 1k responses, avg tokens per conversation, 95th/99th latency, inference cost per 10k tokens.
- Early results (week 1): precision on rerank tests within 2% of baseline, embeddings cosine delta OK; avg latency down ~18% in NA, cost per 10k tokens ~12% lower on hosted config. 95/99 tails still under investigation for long-context API calls.

Technical asks from Cinderpath engineering:
- Redwood compat layer options (OpenAI-style endpoints) or light SDK shim to avoid rewriting client code.
- Controlled canary routing: start 5% traffic to Redwood Hosted during business hours only.
- Prompt parity tools: diff-run harness + automated regression alerts (they want fail-fast).
- KV cache persistence/behavior for chat state across retries and reconnects.
- Encryption/KMS and certs for Dedicated (timing for HSM integration important).

Commercial/Procurement notes:
- They prefer to start on Hosted with a short-term credit and then commit to Dedicated after a successful 3-month POC (expected Q2). Ask for a pricing schedule that maps to a 12-month reserved commit with burstable capacity.
- Procurement flagged need for a clear migration rollback plan and a definition of acceptable model divergence/regression SLA.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-01-20: Intro call - AE + SE - high level requirements captured (FF-20260225-7129).
- 2026-02-04: Demo of Hosted API + console; quick perf numbers shown.
- 2026-02-16: POC kickoff, shared evaluation harness and baseline dataset (Drive doc).
- 2026-02-18: Security questionnaire returned; asked for KMS and audit log samples (FF-20260218-6841).
- 2026-03-02: Mid-POC sync; embedding parity looks good, 95th latency variability noted in EU region.
- 2026-03-09: Pricing follow-up email from AE with 3 commit tiers; Cinderpath asked for a customized Dedicated estimate with specific GPU class and retention settings.

Quotes from stakeholders:
- "If prompt outputs diverge we lose product trust quickly — we need an automated regression gate before cutting traffic." — Head of Product.
- "Cost savings are meaningful, but uptime + predictable latency are why we'd move to Dedicated." — VP Eng.

Next steps (short):
- Deliver dedicated commitment pricing matrix by 2026-03-14.
- SE to run long-tail latency tests for long-context flows in EU region (end by 2026-03-20).
- Legal to provide redlined DPA + data residency clause; sync 2026-03-17.
- Schedule canary rollout plan review with CSM/Ops if POC OK.

Open blockers (notes):
- Need clear definition of "prompt parity" thresholds and acceptable delta for model outputs.
- Peering window: CIO requires VPC peering during maintenance window that currently conflicts with their nightly jobs.
- Legal: confirmation on subprocessors & EU data residency certification timeline.

Contact list: Ethan Park (AE), Maya Lopez (SE), Jordan Kim (CSM), CTO: Priya Raman (Cinderpath), Head of Product: Alex Mercer (Cinderpath).

source.ttft_targets: TTFT < 40ms median for single-line completions; tail 99p < 120ms; jitter (stddev) < 10ms
source.notes:
Summary: DevStream.io builds an IDE copilot product (VSCode/JetBrains) aimed at professional developer workflows. Main ask: enable ultra-low-latency streaming for inline completions and preserve smooth typing UX when network/compute contention occurs.

POC observations (shorthand):
- baseline streaming via hosted API: median TTFT ~120ms, visible jitter when multiple tabs/clients open.
- POC on small dedicated cluster: median TTFT ~45ms, but occasional spikes (100-200ms) when garbage collection/quantized model page-ins happen.
- Inline UX expectations: suggestions should begin streaming within keystroke latency budget (developer perceived TTFT ~ <50ms).
- Token budget tuning: want per-dev soft cap + backpressure semantics (degrade to smaller snippets, not hard failures). Example: 20-token quick completions inline; full-block completions use >100 token budget with explicit user gesture.

Technical asks / feature wishlist (from Priya + Marco):
- streaming chunk size control (stream every 4-8 tokens) and early token flushing to reduce TTFT.
- deterministic model pinning per customer + fast rollback to smaller quantized variant when load high.
- built-in backpressure: return truncated completion with hint ("continue?" button) vs. blocking the editor.
- jitter metrics surfaced in Console: per-route p50/p95/p99 token latency, plus in-flight queue depth and KV cache hit rate.
- per-developer token budgets, cost attribution at token-level in real time.

Security/compliance notes:
- InfoSec: need proof that token logs can be redacted and KMS keys managed by customer (prefer bring-your-own KMS).
- Residency: not strict now but may request EU-only routing for EU customers.

Quote from CTO Marco Reyes: "If our inline suggestions don't feel instantaneous we lose trust. We need consistency more than raw throughput."

Open questions / risks:
- Cost model for always-on low-latency pools (dedicated vs hosted warm instances).
- How much server-side batching can reduce unit cost without violating per-dev latency SLOs.
- Timeline for SAML integration and SOC2 evidence.

Next immediate steps: schedule 90-minute performance deep-dive with runtime team (Priya + Redwood infra), provide dedicated cluster sizing and price, and deliver a short config doc for token-budgeted fallback policies.

- POC on small dedicated cluster: median TTFT ~45ms, but occasional spikes (100-200ms) when garbage collection/quantized model page-ins happen.
- Inline UX expectations: suggestions should begin streaming within keystroke latency budget (developer perceived TTFT ~ <50ms).
- Token budget tuning: want per-dev soft cap + backpressure semantics (degrade to smaller snippets, not hard failures). Example: 20-token quick completions inline; full-block completions use >100 token budget with explicit user gesture.

Technical asks / feature wishlist (from Priya + Marco):
- streaming chunk size control (stream every 4-8 tokens) and early token flushing to reduce TTFT.
- deterministic model pinning per customer + fast rollback to smaller quantized variant when load high.
- built-in backpressure: return truncated completion with hint ("continue?" button) vs. blocking the editor.
- jitter metrics surfaced in Console: per-route p50/p95/p99 token latency, plus in-flight queue depth and KV cache hit rate.
- per-developer token budgets, cost attribution at token-level in real time.

Security/compliance notes:
- InfoSec: need proof that token logs can be redacted and KMS keys managed by customer (prefer bring-your-own KMS).
- Residency: not strict now but may request EU-only routing for EU customers.

Quote from CTO Marco Reyes: "If our inline suggestions don't feel instantaneous we lose trust. We need consistency more than raw throughput."

Open questions / risks:
- Cost model for always-on low-latency pools (dedicated vs hosted warm instances).
- How much server-side batching can reduce unit cost without violating per-dev latency SLOs.
- Timeline for SAML integration and SOC2 evidence.

Next immediate steps: schedule 90-minute performance deep-dive with runtime team (Priya + Redwood infra), provide dedicated cluster sizing and price, and deliver a short config doc for token-budgeted fallback policies.

source.notes:
Overview:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS (B2B), multi-tenant. Want to replace VendorX API for chat summarization + embeddings and move to Redwood Hosted first, then Dedicated once performance/cost targets validated.
- Primary ask: drop-in replacement for OpenAI-compatible flows, prompt parity, stable streaming, and identical behavior for stop sequences + function-calling.
Key non-functional requirements:
- Latency: 80th pctile < 350ms for 256-token chat requests in primary region.
- Throughput: burst to 70 rps for support-bot loads, steady 10–15 rps.
- Cost sensitivity: target <= 20% increase in monthly inference spend on Hosted during POC; Dedicated must show 30–50% unit cost reduction vs current committed pricing.
- Security: SSO (SAML), KMS integration, audit logs retained 1 year, EU data residency for customer EU tenants.
Migration concerns raised on calls:
- Tokenization differences: some prompts show longer token counts with Redwood model variants than VendorX; leads to marginal regressed outputs. Need tokenization parity check and recommended prompt rewrites.
- Function calling parity: VendorX handles nested JSON in tool responses differently; POC will include function-calling parity tests.
- Embeddings drift: small but measurable cosine distance shift on vendor switch for their fine-tuned embeddings. Want evaluation harness to compare retrieval hit-rate on canonical test set.
- Streaming behavior: partial delta ordering changes observed during demo; breaks client streaming parsers. SE to propose compatibility header or streaming mode toggle.
- Regeneration determinism: same prompt+seed returns slightly different summaries vs vendor. Investigate sampling/temperature mapping and top_p equivalences.
POC plan (agreed):
- Week 0: narrow scope, 10 prompts and 2 retrieval tasks chosen; share test harness (Drive link).
- Week 1: Redwood Hosted endpoints, run A/B vs VendorX on 50k tokens, collect metric set (latency, token usage, retrieval precision, user-acceptance).
- Week 2: Prompt parity fixes & rerun; escalate regressions to model/profile recommendations or warmed KV cache experiments.
- Post-POC: pricing conversation for Dedicated sizing (target: 4x T4 equivalent GPUs or Managed nv4 per capacity) and SLA.
Commercial notes:
- They are on a renewal with VendorX in Q3; looking to move before renewal if POC is clean.
- Procurement is sensitive to minimum commit for Dedicated; procurement wants a phased commitment with runway to expand.
Action items from last call (03/04):
- SE Ethan: provide tokenization parity checklist and lightweight shim examples for streaming compatibility (assigned, due 03/08).
- AE Maya: send initial Hosted pricing scenario + estimate for 2-week Hosted POC (sent 03/05).
- Security: Redwood security FAQ requested (Drive link).
- Avenryx to provide sample prompt set + reference retrieval corpus by 03/09.
Quotable lines from customer:
- "We need a provider that won't surprise our product engineers — prompt changes can't silently change behavior in prod."
- "If you can prove cost parity and fewer regressions, we'll consider Dedicated for next fiscal year."

notes: Inbound from CTO (Ethan Park) — building a VS Code copilot extension used by their internal dev teams and beta customers.
Primary ask: streaming completions with character-level low-latency (dev experience: inline suggestions as you type) — p99 target < 120ms for local devs in NA.
Concern: current hosted vendors throttle per-token and per-connection; they need predictable per-developer rate limits and team quotas to avoid noisy neighbors when 200+ devs sync heavy repos.

Model/version pinning is a hard requirement: QA wants deterministic suggestions with a pinned model for productized features. Also need automated fallback to a quantized model when latency spikes or cost thresholds hit.
Auth & SSO: SAML required, SCIM for user provisioning desirable. IT asked about session lifetime, token refresh flow for long-running editor sessions (VS Code keeps sockets open).
Security: must keep audit logs for 13 months, ability to TTL code snippets, and KMS integration for customer-managed keys (HSM optional).
Edge routing: they'd prefer region-aware routing to keep devs in NA on NA-hosted inference, failover to EU if NA saturated. Interested in autoscaling burst capacity for peak CI runs.

Dev notes from PoC kickoff: install VSIX, set env REDWOOD_API_KEY, verify streaming endpoint responds with chunked events. Reference snippet they used in extension: streaming over SSE with reconnection/backoff.
Quote from Ethan: 'We need responses that feel instantaneous in the editor — anything more than 200ms feels laggy for inline completions.'
Cost sensitivity: willing to pay for dedicated capacity for predictable latency but wants Redwood Optimize suggestions to reduce token spend (batching cached prefixes across repo contexts).
Compliance: draft NDA signed; security questionnaire in progress — submitted Drive doc (see linked_drive_docs).
Potential rollout plan: beta with 50 internal devs -> 200 devs -> customer beta; target GA in Q3 if POC meets latency and SSO requirements.
SE action items: provide sample metrics dashboard for token consumption per-IDE-instance, outline rate-limit semantics for streaming connections and reconnection strategies.
Last demo (2026-03-02): showed streaming completion with fallback; notes: p50=45ms p95=130ms in demo infra, need tuning to hit p99 <120ms with real workload.
They requested an example VS Code auth flow doc and a suggested retry/backoff policy for SSE/websocket streaming endpoints.
They have an internal Jira for extension bugs — suggested mapping Redwood incident keys to their INF- tickets for traceability.
Open question: can Redwood expose per-extension instance token/quota tracking and per-team throttles?

source.notes:
Summary: Aurum Horizon operates route-optimization and real-time ETA services for large commercial fleets. They're evaluating Redwood Dedicated with multi-region reserved pools to guarantee throughput during peak routing windows and to meet contractual latency SLOs for enterprise customers.

Key customer quotes / snippets:
- 'We need predictable throughput for morning/evening load spikes — can't have degraded routing for 10k+ vessels.'
- 'Failover between us-east-1 and us-west-2 must be seamless; 15–20% headroom required.'

POC observations (short):
- Baseline (2-day): 120 reqs/s sustained on a mixed batch profile (avg payload 400 tokens); 95th pct latency for generation ~140ms using baseline A100 configs.
- Observed token distribution skewed: 60% short ( <128 ), 30% medium (128–512), 10% long (512–2048) — affects batching/windowing.

Commercial / procurement notes:
- Want reserved GPU pools across 2 regions (us-east, us-west) with Active-Passive failover; prefer mirrored capacity for EU pilot later.
- Target ARR band ~ $500k-$1M; 2-year commit preferred for pricing.

Internal ask from SE/AE:
- Need detailed per-AZ GPU counts and confirmation of headroom math for 99.95% availability target.
- Legal wants an explicit RTO for cross-AZ failover; procurement asked for credits table.

Stenography / misc:
- Drive links shared; awaiting answers to security matrix (drive:/security/aurum-security-questions.xlsx).
- Mentioned competitors evaluating: OpenAI enterprise endpoints and a self-hosted Kubernetes + Triton stack.
source.technical_requirements:
Throughput & capacity: target sustained throughput 300 reqs/s at 95th pct latency <= 200ms (generation step) during peak windows; plan initial reserved pool = 48x GPUs (A100-80GB equivalent) split across 2 regions: us-east-1 (primary) = 30 GPUs across 3 AZs (10/10/10), us-west-2 (secondary) = 18 GPUs across 2 AZs (9/9) for passive failover. Headroom: 20% additional burst capacity (hot spare) reserved per-region to meet SLA.

SLO / SLA asks: availability 99.95% for Dedicated endpoints, failover RTO <= 5 min for AZ-level outage with automatic routing to secondary region; credit schedule requested for sustained outages >30 minutes.

Model & routing: prefer Llama-3-class variants quantized; will accept Redwood-managed compatible fallbacks (smaller quantized variants) for emergency degrade. Require model pinning per rollout.

Operational: metrics export to Aurum's Datadog; token-level billing breakdown; per-route tracing and per-model dashboards. Audit logs + KMS integration mandatory.

POC / next milestones: 2-week extended load test (simulate morning peak x10 load), validate 95th pct latency with batching and KV cache configs; deliver final reserved GPU sizing spreadsheet and draft SLA for procurement by 2026-04-15.

Summary: mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood as primary inference layer. Started on Hosted API trial (Q1 POC), now running dedicated demo + security review for Dedicated/VPC.

Recent timeline:
- 2026-01-20: Intro call (Ethan) — product scope: customer support bot + doc search + embeddings for similarity ranking.
- 2026-02-05: Hosted API POC began — ingestion of 600k tokens, basic prompt templates, initial latency ~220ms p95 on hosted.
- 2026-02-18: Deep demo of Dedicated features (Priya, runtime eng). Fireflies ff_8421_ambergrove_demo_2026-02-18.
- 2026-02-24: POC throughput test — target 200 rps, goal p95 < 150ms; Redwood hosted passed functional correctness but need Dedicated for predictable throughput.
- 2026-03-02: Security intake submitted (security@ambergrove). Expect SOC2 evidence + SAML details.
- 2026-03-08: Pricing walkthrough with finance — prefer monthly committed Dedicated pool, conservative burst; asked for model/quantization delta breakdown.
- Ongoing: network team waiting on VPC peering checklist from Redwood infra; legal flagged EU residency clause.

Use-case & requirements snapshot:
- Workloads: mix of chat (support agent), reranking and embeddings for doc search.
- Latency: target p95 <= 150ms for chat, bulk embedding jobs can be async.
- Throughput: steady 120-200 rps during business hours, spiky bursts to 600 rps.
- Cost sensitivity: mid-market constraints — want predictable committed pricing + burst caps.
- Model preferences: open models preferred (cost + control), but must match quality of current hosted baseline; interested in quantized variants and Redwood Optimize recommendations.
- Routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to smaller model variant in outages and regional failover across NA/EU.

Security / compliance notes:
- Require SOC2 attestation before procurement sign-off (legal).
- SSO: SAML 2.0 required; engineering to run SAML test with Redwood dev/staging (scheduled).
- Audit logs: need per-request audit logging + retention controls (retain logs 13 months).
- KMS: must support customer-managed keys (KMS/HSM) for production data keys.
- Data residency: EU-customer data must stay in EU regions; contract language requested.

POC milestones & metrics:
- Functional: conversion from hosted templates to Dedicated containerized runtime (done).
- Performance: p95 latency target testing (next: Dedicated pool benchmark 2026-03-16).
- Security: SAML smoke test + SOC2 artifact delivery (target 2026-03-20).
- Cost: finalize ARR band (expected 50k-150k) and committed GPU pool size.

Quotes / call snippets:
- "We like the unified API — want to avoid re-architecting if we move to Dedicated." — Head of Product
- "Finance needs a clear committed vs burst pricing example; show delta if we use quantized models." — CFO

Links / artifacts:
- Pricing deck: drive:pricing/dedicated_proposal_ambergrove_v1.pdf
- Security FAQ + SOC2 Qs: drive:security/soc2_responses_ambergrove.md
- Support tickets: RED-1247 (network peering), RED-1303 (KMS integration questions)
- Gmail threads for contract/pricing: thread/u/0?msgid=1782938472, thread/u/1?msgid=1783958823

Next steps (short):
- Run SAML test with AmberGrove IdP (Priya + AmberGrove infra) week of 2026-03-15.
- Deliver Dedicated benchmark report and updated pricing by 2026-03-17.
- Legal to review data residency clause after SOC2 artifacts received.

Sales notes (AE): keep comms tight with infra and legal — biggest stallers are SOC2 evidence and VPC peering checklist. If both cleared, advance to procurement and PO within 30 days.

source.next_step: Run 2-week POC: canary model pin + eval gate; infra deep-dive scheduled 2026-03-14
source.notes:
POC goal: deterministic, reproducible code completions inside Arclytic IDE plugin with safe progressive rollouts.

- Background: Arclytic builds an in-IDE copiloting product used by internal dev teams + ~80 beta customers. Concern: unpredictable model updates changed completion rank/order and caused flaky build scripts. Wants model/version pinning + rollbacks.
- Key asks (from PM & platform lead): pin model version per release channel, reproducible sampling (seed), ability to run canaries split by user cohort, automated eval gates before each rollout.
- Acceptance metrics they care about:
  • compile-failure delta <= 0.2% vs baseline (reject if higher)
  • 95th pctl latency target < 120ms for inline completions (low-latency streaming is critical)
  • token-level quality regression threshold: no more than 3% BLEU/EDR drop on curated prompt set
  • rollback if user-reported error rate > 0.5% or automated guardrails tripped
- Desired rollout mechanics:
  • Canary at 5% of users (team-level flags), monitor metrics for 48h
  • Gate: automated evaluation harness runs on commit to model catalog; must pass quality + perf & return signed gate token
  • Gradual ramp: 5% -> 20% -> 60% -> 100% with automated rollback triggers
- Reproducibility requirements:
  • model_version stamped in completion metadata, session IDs persisted to logs and traceable to artifact store
  • deterministic sampling options for CI (temp=0, top_k/top_p pinned) and ability to replay sessions against older model binaries for bisecting
  • artifact retention: store candidate completions + inputs for 90 days (security needs opt-out)
- Integration points they want from Redwood:
  • SDK hook to pin model + sampling config at request time
  • per-deployment rollouts (dedicated pools) with canary routing and automatic fallback to pinned older model if latency spikes
  • evaluation-as-a-service: run their custom prompt set against model candidates and return pass/fail and diffs
- Timeline & recent activity:
  • 2026-02-18: initial intro call (Jordan) — high level feature interest, demo request
  • 2026-02-25: security questionnaire submitted (see Drive doc)
  • 2026-03-02: deep dive with infra + product (Fireflies FF-2026-03-02-8672) — confirmed need for VPC + KMS integration
  • 2026-03-05: demo of streaming and pin APIs (SE: Maya) — positive feedback; asked for canary flow example
  • 2026-03-08: legal sent initial data residency Qs; product requested POC acceptance criteria
  • 2026-03-14: infra workshop + POC kickoff (tentative)

- Quotes:
  • "We need deterministic completions for build scripts — model drift breaks CI runs." — Lead Platform Eng
  • "Canaries must be first-class, not a manual config we forget about." — Head of Product

- Risks: audit log retention limits may conflict with artifact retention; perf SLA on dedicated pools needs verification.

Short next steps (CRM): schedule infra workshop, deliver example canary rollout config + sample eval gate, confirm EU residency options for artifact storage.

source.notes:
Snapshot notes and call fragments:
- Customer profile: mid-market SaaS focused on B2B product analytics (search across docs, changelogs, support notes).
- Primary drivers: improve search relevance and surface related product docs via semantic embeddings; reduce cost per query at scale.
- Quote from CTO: 'We need sub-150ms median for question -> top-K; batch jobs can be slower but cost must be predictable.'
- Product lead emphasized: 'Nightly reindex is fine for bulk, but hot updates need <15m freshness for new release notes.'
- They began with Hosted API to iterate quickly; now assessing Dedicated because of predictable throughput and VPC controls.
- Security: SOC2 yes, SSO/SAML mandatory, KMS for key control, audit logs for query provenance, potential EU residency for a subset of customer data.
- Risk: index growth (expected to reach 12M vectors in 9 months) could blow embedding & storage spend unless we tune cardinality/quantization.
- Internal stakeholders: CTO (Aman Rao), Head of Product (Leah Morton), Infra Lead (Diego Torres).
- Notes shorthand: "POC shows promise — relevance + fewer false positives, but cost model TBD"
source.poc_details:
POC scope and measurement plan:
- Objective: prove embeddings + semantic search will increase top-3 click-through and keep median query latency <= 150ms at 200 qps target.
- Data: 3M documents (product docs, changelogs, support transcriptions).
- Embedding models tested: open-embedding-small-v2 (fast, cheaper) vs open-embedding-large-v1 (higher quality).
- Vector DBs: started with Pinecone (managed) for speed of prototyping; parallel test on self-hosted Milvus on k8s (Dedicated-like ops).
- Ingest pattern: nightly bulk batch (1.2M new vectors/night in largest runs), incremental hot updates every 15 minutes via stream.
- Measurements captured: latency p50/p95/p99, recall@10, index storage cost, embedding compute cost per 1M docs, batch throughput (docs/sec).
- Intermediate POC finding: batch embedding pipeline can sustain ~6k docs/sec with current worker fleet; nightly window fits but cost per embed with hosted embeddings needs tuning.
- Next technical ask: show Dedicated config with reserved GPU pool + quantization profile to hit 2-3x cost reduction vs hosted at scale.
- Acceptance criteria: recall@10 improvement >= 12% vs current BM25 baseline AND sustained p95 latency < 400ms at 200 qps for top-K queries.

Key constraints: frequent re-index (nightly + nearline deltas), commits produce many small updates, want incremental indexing APIs.
Perf target: 95th pctile query latency < 120ms for top-k (k=10) across global users; tail latency important.
Cost target: target < $0.01/query on average; finance will push back if > $0.03.
Model preference: open 2k-8k token encoder models (1536–4096 dim), test both dense and hybrid rerank approaches.
Routing/fallback: want automatic fallback to lower-cost region or quantized index during bursts; regional data residency for EU customers.
Security: SSO/SAML for developer portal, KMS integration mandatory, retention controls for PII removed from repo metadata.
Quote from lead eng: "We need predictable unit costs and an ops story — batching+caching suggestions are big win."
Internal note: evaluate Redwood Optimize recommendations vs our current batching layer; measure delta on token costs.
POC setup: sample corpus (10M files) uploaded to S3, generated 12M embeddings at 1536d for current test cycle.
Ask for Redwood: clear docs on reindex API, delta-only ingest patterns, estimated storage + query cost for 120M vectors.
SE feedback: likely to require Dedicated for sustained throughput; test Hosted for MVP latency profile.

Summary: Mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood. Initial onboarding via hosted API to validate latency/quality, scoped move to Dedicated (VPC) for production. Security review underway (SOC2 + SSO + audit logs + KMS).

Discovery highlights:
- Customer profile: B2B SaaS (product analytics SDK + workspace), NA HQ, EU users require residency.
- Primary workloads: chat (customer-facing assistant in-app), embeddings for search across event data, reranking for top-N results.
- Traffic profile: steady baseline ~150 qps embeddings/search, bursty chat peaks ~400 qps during release windows. Cost-sensitive for bursts.
- Latency targets: chat p95 < 200ms for 512-token flows, embedding gen < 80ms single-vector on avg.
- Model preference: prefer Llama-3-class 70B quantized int8 on Dedicated; acceptable fallback to 13B for lower-cost routes. Want routing policy: prefer low-latency (dedicated region) then fallback to hosted cheaper variant.

POC / eval plan (agreed):
- Phase 1 (hosted, 2 weeks): validate prompt templates, obs via Redwood Console, token-level cost breakdown. (Completed: Feb 2026)
- Phase 2 (dedicated pilot, 4 weeks): reserved 2x A100-equivalent, VPC peering test, SAML SSO integration with Okta, audit log streaming to their SIEM. (Planned start: 2026-03-15)
- Success criteria: chat p95 <200ms, embedding latency <80ms, no data exfil in VPC tests, cost per 1M tokens within agreed band.

Security / compliance notes (what Legal/Infra asked for):
- Must provide SOC2 Type II report + subprocessor list (legal won't sign until received).
- SSO: Okta IdP metadata, SAML integration docs, SCIM or Just-in-Time provisioning desirable.
- Audit logs: retention configurable, prefer 12+ months; need streaming to customer SIEM (Splunk).
- KMS: customer-managed key support required for Dedicated (bring-your-own-key) or HSM integration.
- Data residency: EU-in-region hosting for EU customers; confirm region routing and fallbacks.
- Retention controls: ability to set per-route retention and automatic purge for PII-ish traces.

Commercial / pricing convo:
- Asking for committed capacity discount tiers (monthly commit) vs on-demand hosted token rates.
- Wants model to include burst capacity clauses and an overage policy.
- Redwood asked for ARR estimate to size proposal: CF expects $125k-$250k ARR if SLA and residency checks out.

- "If latency is a hair over target on peak, we need automated fallback to cheaper instance to avoid failed requests." — CTO

- Schedule SOC2 evidence review call (target 2026-03-16) and dedicated pilot kickoff (2026-03-15 tentative).

Account background: Copperleaf is a programmatic ad exchange focusing on sub-50ms decision loops for bidder logic and creative ranking. They process ~5k-15k QPS at peak across multiple regions. Interested in Redwood for predictable tail latency and unit-cost reduction.

- "If we can't hit tail latency at scale, bids lose money" — Evan Morales (Head of Ads).
- Strong interest in: automated batching controls (sticky per-route), prefix/KV caching for repeated creative templates, quantization for production cost savings but need quality guardrails.
- Security: must integrate with AWS KMS, SSO (Okta/SAML), SOC2 mandatory, US data residency.
- Budget: target ARR for vendor is $200k-$500k; procurement scheduled after successful POC and security audit.
- Questions outstanding: how Redwood measures warm vs cold cache tail, support for hybrid routing (dedicated + hosted fallback), and SLA for canary rollbacks.

source.notes:
Account snapshot: mid-market B2B SaaS (user-facing search + chat embedded in product). Product team is pragmatic — wants to move from Hosted API proof to Dedicated reserved capacity to control unit economics and predictable latency. Key asks: deterministic p95/p99 under 100ms for short chat turns, 10k qps/day baseline for embeddings pipeline (batching allowed), predictable cost per 1k tokens, and audit trails for regulated customers.

POC plan (agreed high level): 4-week Dedicated POC. Objectives: demonstrate stable p99 latency under load, quantify tokens-per-second cost with batching/caching, validate canary routing and automated rollback. Canary plan: start with 10% production traffic -> 30% -> 60% -> 100% over 10 days given no regressions. Canary metrics gated: error rate < 0.5%, p99 latency within 1.2x of baseline, quality regression < 1.5% on golden prompt set.

Fallback / routing strategy: primary serving on Dedicated us-west cluster; secondary region us-east for regional failover; model-variant fallback to a quantized 8-bit variant (faster/lower-cost) when Dedicated capacity is constrained; final fallback to Hosted API for very bursty spikes. Routing will be policy-driven: latency-first for premium routes, cost-first for batch jobs. Automated rollback: if canary metrics breach thresholds for 3 consecutive 5-min windows -> auto rollback to previous model version and route traffic to Hosted API while alerting SE/AE.

Model variants: full fp16 for highest quality (default for chat premium tier), int8/quantized for cost-sensitive reranking and background embeddings jobs. SE Jordan will run perf matrix comparing fp16 vs quantized on their representative prompts.

Security/compliance: legal requires data residency within US for customer subset; SSO (SAML) and KMS integration mandatory. Redwood to provide SOC 2 attestation and audit log retention policy. CinderPath asked for retention controls configurable per-route.

Commercials discussed: committed Dedicated pool proposal at $XXk/month (mid-market tier) with burst credits. AE to circulate SOW and two pricing options (12-month commit vs 3-month pilot uplift). CinderPath prefers 3-month pilot followed by annual commit if POC meets SLAs.

Quotes / direct notes from call: "We need deterministic latency for in-app chat — UX is unforgiving." — Head of Product. "Can we fail back to Hosted quickly if Dedicated flaps?" — CTO.

Next steps (short): AE to send SOW + pricing (by 2026-03-12). SE to deliver perf matrix and canary runbook (by 2026-03-15). Legal exchange: security checklist to be returned by 2026-03-20. Internal procurement window ends 2026-04-05.

Summary: Large financial analytics provider building RAG over internal research + regulatory filings. Target index 200M–800M vectors initially; plan growth to 1B+ within 12–18 months. Primary workload: embeddings + two-stage rerank (ANN -> cross-attention reranker).

Risks: procurement calendar (decision Q2), long security review (legal wants HSM/KMS details), and on-prem infra readiness for 1B vector sharding.

source.notes:
Top-line: Atlas Scale Retrieval is a global media-archive company (news video + audio transcripts) migrating decades of content into a RAG stack. They expect 500M–750M vectors initially, long-term 1B+. Primary workloads: semantic search + embedding-based reranking for relevance and deduplication. Key metrics: target 10k steady-state QPS (spiky up to 30k), p50 <= 40-60ms for initial retrieval+rerank pipeline, p95 SLA < 300ms for customer-facing search, cost sensitivity: target unit cost $0.0003-0.001/query.

Technical notes / preferences:
- Embeddings: prefer a two-tier approach: dense 1536/3072-d embedding for retrieval (open model or Redwood-curated), and smaller candidate sets to feed cross-encoder reranker (DeBERTa/adapter-based cross-encoder) for final scoring.
- Indexing scale: plan for 500M vectors now, schema for shards + multi-region replication. Interested in Redwood recommendations for quantization (4/8-bit) + PQ to hit cost targets.
- Latency: must support aggressive batching and KV/prefix caching for hot prefixes (e.g., breaking news). Also need dynamic routing to fall back to cheaper model variants during peak.
- Throughput/cost controls: need intelligent batching suggestions, automatic cache warming for morning spikes, and per-customer routing policies.
- Security: must integrate with customer KMS and SSO (SAML); audit logs retention 7 years for legal/regulatory requirements. On-prem option required for EU contracts.

Interactions:
- AE Jordan leading commercial; SE Riya running technical bench. Legal + security heavily involved; finance needs clearer TCO for Dedicated vs Private. POC scope: validate 1M subset at production QPS profile, then 7-day soak on Dedicated with realistic traffic.

Quoted customer quote captured in call: "We can’t pay for cheap embeddings if rerank kills latency — need the sweet spot between recall and unit cost."
source.poc_results_summary: 1M-subset ingest: retrieval recall @k=100 = 0.87; cross-encoder rerank improved top-10 AR = +14 pts; observed 6k qps steady with 50ms p95 on single optimised shard. Need linear scale factors and network peering to meet 10k-30k targets.

technical_requirements: Throughput guarantee: sustained 450 qps for chat front-end for 3 hours/day
Latency SLO: p50 < 40ms, p95 < 120ms, p99 < 200ms for token generation on primary model
Burst capacity: ability to handle 1.8x baseline for up to 30 minutes without request rejection
KV/prefix cache warm-up: pre-warmed for expected conversation cohorts to avoid cold-start latency
Model fallback: automatic routing to quantized variant with graceful quality degradation
Audit logs: full request/response metadata retention for 90 days (redacted PII)
preferred_sla_terms: Latency SLOs in appendix tied to service credits
99.95% uptime for Dedicated pool control plane

Commit term: 12 months with quarterly throughput reviews
capacity_plan: Reserved GPU pool sized for baseline 450 qps using N4 GPU equivalent (TBD after pilot benchmarking)
Burst buffer: additional shared GPUs available via dedicated burst capacity (billing: usage over commit)
Warm pool: 10% of reserved nodes kept pre-warmed during market open hours, 2% off-hours
Pre-provisioning windows: scale-up scheduled 15 minutes before expected peak (configurable cron)
autoscaling_policy_proposal: Scale-up trigger: CPU/GPU-steal + request queue depth sustained > 60s percentile for 3 consecutive 30s windows
Scale-down trigger: sustained utilization < 30% for 10 minutes and queue depth < 5
Min nodes: equal to warm-pool size (default 10% of commit)
Max nodes: commit + 50% burst cap (contract negotiable)
Cooldowns: 120s for scale-up to avoid oscillation, 900s for scale-down to avoid rapid churn
Warm-pool lifecycle: keep model KV caches intact during warm-pool idle periods; prefer hibernation over full teardown
queueing_behavior_notes: Prefer tail-drop with priority lanes: interactive chat gets priority over background embedding jobs
Soft-queueing with estimated wait feedback to client for requests beyond target latency
Request routing: small-token requests routed to low-latency lane; long-context requests can be queued or routed to cheaper quantized variant
Backpressure policy: early rejection threshold + 503 with retry-after header for graceful client handling

source.canary_strategy: Start at 5% of mirrored traffic; monitor p50/p95, token cost, hallucination rate on eval set. If any of: p95 > 600ms, error rate >1%, or quality regression >5% on prompts -> pause and rollback to hosted or previous model variant. After 7 days of stable metrics, increase to 25% then 50%, final cutover gated by SE sign-off.
source.fallback_routing_plan: Primary: dedicated us-west-2 cluster. On degraded cluster health (CPU/GPU overload or >1% errors for 5m) failover to compatible model variant in eu-west-1 with auto-traffic-shift. Fallback uses smaller model variant to preserve latency. All traffic during failover served under reduced throughput limits and throttling applied. Post-failover: automated incident ticket creation (Jira) and rollback window 24h.
